name: Kuwaiti Balaleet (Sweet Saffron Rice with Egg)

ingredients.note: standing in for the traditional vermicelli

steps: Cook the rice until tender, then toss over low heat with the sugar and a saffron-steeped splash of water until glossy and caramelized.
Make a thin omelet with the egg and lay it over the top.

ingredients_full.note: standing in for the traditional vermicelli

steps_detailed: Cook the rice until tender, then toss over low heat with the sugar, cardamom, and a saffron-and-rosewater-steeped splash of water, 5-6 minutes, until glossy and fragrant.
Make a thin, well-seasoned omelet with the egg and lay it over the top.
Balaleet is a weekend-morning fixture in Kuwaiti homes, often served alongside a strong cup of Arabic coffee.

name: Kuwaiti Chebab with Date and Ghee
steps: Whisk the flour, egg, and milk into a smooth, pourable batter.
Cook small rounds on a hot, lightly oiled griddle until bubbles form, then flip briefly.
Mash the dates with a splash of water into a rough syrup and drizzle over the warm pancakes.
steps_detailed: Whisk the flour, egg, milk, and cardamom into a smooth, pourable batter and rest 10 minutes.
Cook small, thick rounds on a hot, lightly oiled griddle, 2-3 minutes per side, until deeply golden and set.
Blend the mashed dates with the melted ghee and a splash of warm water into a rough syrup, and drizzle generously over the warm chebab.

name: Kuwaiti Egg and Date Breakfast
steps: Fry the eggs in the ghee.
Serve with the dates on the side.
steps_detailed: Fry the eggs in the ghee over medium heat, seasoning with black pepper to taste.
Serve with the dates alongside - a simple, energizing breakfast eaten across Kuwaiti households before the day gets going.

name: Kuwaiti Chicken Machboos
steps: Brown the chicken with the onion.
Add the tomatoes and 1.5 cups water, and simmer 15 minutes.
Add the rice, cover, and simmer 18-20 more minutes until tender.
steps_detailed: Brown the chicken with the onion, garlic, turmeric, and cardamom over medium-high heat, 8-10 minutes.
Add the diced tomatoes and 1.75 cups water and simmer 18-20 minutes to build the base.
Add the rice, cover, and simmer 20-25 more minutes until tender and richly colored.
Kuwaiti machboos is often held up as the definitive version of the dish across the Gulf, built on a specific blend of dried spices called bzar.

name: Kuwaiti Fish Machboos
steps: Saute the onion until softened.
Add 1.5 cups water and the rice, cover, and simmer 15 minutes.
Lay the fish over the rice, cover, and steam 8-10 more minutes until cooked through. Finish with the lime juice.
steps_detailed: Saute the onion, garlic, and turmeric until softened and fragrant, 5-6 minutes.
Add 1.75 cups water and the rice, cover, and simmer 18-20 minutes.
Lay the fish over the rice, cover, and steam 10-12 more minutes until just cooked through and flaking.
Finish with the lime juice - fish machboos traces back to Kuwait's pearling and dhow-trading past, when the sea, not oil, was the center of the economy.

name: Kuwaiti Margoog (Vegetable Stew with Dough Diamonds)
steps: Simmer the chicken with the potato and tomatoes until nearly tender, about 20 minutes.
Make a simple, firm dough from the flour and water, roll thin, and cut into small diamonds.
Drop the dough diamonds into the simmering stew and cook 8-10 more minutes until tender and slightly thickened.
steps_detailed: Simmer the chicken with the potato, tomatoes, onion, and cumin in a splash of water for 22-25 minutes until nearly tender.
Make a simple, firm dough from the flour, a pinch of salt, and water, roll thin, and cut into small diamond shapes.
Drop the dough diamonds into the simmering stew and cook 8-10 more minutes until tender, letting them thicken the broth as they cook.
Margoog, with its distinctive cut-dough pieces, is closely associated with Kuwaiti home cooking specifically, distinct from the torn-bread thareed found elsewhere in the Gulf.

name: Kuwaiti Harees
steps: Simmer the chicken with the onion until very tender, then shred it finely, reserving the broth.
Add the bulgur to the broth and simmer, mashing often, until it forms a thick, smooth porridge.
Stir the chicken back in, top with the ghee, and serve.
steps_detailed: Simmer the chicken with the onion in 2.5 cups water for 25-30 minutes until very tender, then shred it finely, reserving the broth.
Add the bulgur to the broth and simmer 25-30 minutes, mashing frequently, until it forms a thick, smooth porridge.
Stir most of the shredded chicken back in, top with the ghee and cinnamon, and serve - harees is cooked in huge shared pots across Kuwait during Ramadan.

name: Kuwaiti Ghuzi (Spiced Lamb over Rice)

ingredients.ingredient: Raisins, dark, seedless (Includes foods for USDA's Food Distribution Program)

steps: Season the lamb with the turmeric and shape into small portions.
Roast at 375F for 15-18 minutes until well browned and cooked through.
Serve over the cooked rice, scattered with the raisins.

ingredients_full.ingredient: Raisins, dark, seedless (Includes foods for USDA's Food Distribution Program)

steps_detailed: Season the lamb thoroughly with the turmeric, garlic, and cardamom, and shape into small portions.
Roast at 375F (190C) for 18-22 minutes, turning once, until deeply browned and tender.
Serve piled over the rice, scattered generously with raisins and pistachios - the traditional centerpiece of Kuwaiti weddings and National Day feasts.

name: Kuwaiti Lamb Machboos
steps: Brown the lamb with the onion, breaking it up as it cooks.
Add the tomatoes and 1.5 cups water, and simmer 15 minutes.
Add the rice, cover, and simmer 18-20 more minutes until tender.
steps_detailed: Brown the lamb with the onion, garlic, turmeric, and paprika over medium-high heat, breaking it up well, 8-10 minutes.
Add the diced tomatoes and 1.75 cups water and simmer 18-20 minutes.
Add the rice, cover, and simmer 20-25 more minutes until tender and richly colored.

name: Kuwaiti Chicken Saloona
steps: Brown the chicken with the onion.
Add the potato, tomatoes, and 1 cup water, and simmer 25-30 minutes until the potato is tender.
steps_detailed: Brown the chicken with the onion, garlic, cumin, and turmeric over medium-high heat, 8-10 minutes.
Add the potato, tomatoes, and 1.25 cups water, and simmer 28-32 minutes until the potato is fully tender.
Saloona is the everyday weeknight dinner across Kuwait, easily stretched with whatever vegetables are in the kitchen.

name: Kuwaiti Grilled Fish with Lime Rice
steps: Rub the fish with the cumin and garlic, and marinate at least 15 minutes.
Grill until charred and cooked through.
Serve over rice cooked with a squeeze of lime.
steps_detailed: Rub the fish with the cumin, garlic, and oil, and marinate at least 20-25 minutes.
Grill over medium-high heat, 5-7 minutes per side, until well charred and cooked through.
Serve over rice cooked with the lime juice - grilled fish remains a weekend staple for many Kuwaiti families along the Gulf coast.

name: Kuwaiti Samboosa
steps: Brown the beef with the onion.
Make a simple dough from the flour and water, roll thin, cut into strips, fill, and fold into triangles.
Fry until golden and crisp.
steps_detailed: Brown the beef with the onion, cumin, and cilantro.
Make a simple, firm dough from the flour, a pinch of salt, and water, and roll out thin.
Cut into strips, fill, and fold into tight triangles, sealing the edges well.
Fry until deeply golden and crisp, 4-5 minutes - samboosa appear by the tray-full at Kuwaiti Ramadan gatherings.

name: Kuwaiti Khubz with Cheese
steps: Warm the bread on a dry pan.
Scatter with the cheese and drizzle with the oil.
Fold and serve warm.
steps_detailed: Warm the bread on a dry pan over medium heat, 1-2 minutes per side.
Scatter generously with the cheese and za'atar, and drizzle with the oil.
Fold in half and press briefly until the cheese softens.

name: Kuwaiti Hummus with Garlic and Lime

ingredients.ingredient: Chickpeas (garbanzo beans, bengal gram), mature seeds, canned, drained solids
Seeds, sesame butter, tahini, from roasted and toasted kernels (most common type)

steps: Blend the chickpeas, tahini, lime juice, and garlic with a splash of water until smooth.
Serve with warm flatbread.

ingredients_full.ingredient: Chickpeas (garbanzo beans, bengal gram), mature seeds, canned, drained solids
Seeds, sesame butter, tahini, from roasted and toasted kernels (most common type)

steps_detailed: Blend the chickpeas, tahini, lime juice, garlic, and cumin with a splash of water until very smooth.
Spoon into a shallow dish and drizzle with the oil.

name: Kuwaiti Regag Crepe with Egg and Cilantro
steps: Whisk the flour with enough water to make a very thin, pourable batter.
Spread thin on a hot, oiled pan, crack the egg over it, and scatter with the cilantro.
Cook until set, fold, and serve.
steps_detailed: Whisk the flour with enough water to make a very thin, almost lacy batter.
Spread thin across a hot, well-oiled pan, crack the egg directly over it, season with black pepper, and scatter with the cilantro.
Cook 2-3 minutes until the edges crisp and lift and the egg sets, then fold into quarters - regag is a familiar quick bite from Kuwait City's older markets.

name: Kuwaiti Legaimat

ingredients.note: tbsp, standing in for date syrup

steps: Mix the flour, baking powder, and enough water to form a thick, smooth batter. Rest 20 minutes.
Drop spoonfuls into hot oil and fry until puffed and golden.
Drizzle with the honey while still warm.

ingredients_full.note: tbsp, standing in for date syrup

steps_detailed: Mix the flour, baking powder, saffron, and enough warm water to form a thick, smooth batter. Cover and rest 25-30 minutes.
Drop spoonfuls into hot oil and fry, turning occasionally, 3-5 minutes until puffed and deeply golden.
Drain briefly and drizzle generously with the honey while still warm - legaimat is a nightly fixture at Kuwaiti Ramadan tents and family iftars.

name: Kuwaiti Khabees (Date and Ghee Pudding)
steps: Toast the flour in the ghee until lightly golden and fragrant.
Stir in the mashed dates, sugar, and a splash of water, and cook until it comes together into a thick, spoonable paste.
steps_detailed: Toast the flour in the ghee over medium-low heat, stirring constantly, 4-5 minutes until lightly golden and nutty-smelling.
Stir in the mashed dates, sugar, and a generous splash of water, and cook 5-7 more minutes, stirring, until it comes together into a thick, dark, spoonable paste.
Dust with the cinnamon before serving - a rich, simple sweet found across Kuwaiti family tables at the close of a meal.

name: Kuwaiti Saffron Milk Pudding
steps: Whisk the cornstarch into a little of the cold milk until smooth.
Warm the rest of the milk with the sugar and saffron, whisk in the cornstarch slurry, and simmer, stirring, until thickened.
Chill before serving.
steps_detailed: Whisk the cornstarch into a little of the cold milk until completely smooth.

Warm the rest of the milk with the sugar, saffron, and rosewater, whisk in the cornstarch slurry, and simmer over medium-low heat, stirring constantly, 5-6 minutes until thickened to a soft, custard-like set.
Chill at least 2 hours and top with the pistachios - a delicately set, fragrant pudding common to close out a Kuwaiti family meal.

name: Kuwaiti Tomato and Cumin Sauce
steps: Mash the tomatoes lightly and combine with the cumin, garlic, and lime juice.
Serve alongside grilled meats or fish.
steps_detailed: Mash the tomatoes lightly and combine with the cumin, garlic, lime juice, and cilantro.
Let sit 10 minutes so the flavors meld - a simple table sauce that turns up alongside machboos and grilled fish alike.

name: Kuwaiti Tahini and Date Drizzle

ingredients.ingredient: Seeds, sesame butter, tahini, from roasted and toasted kernels (most common type)

steps: Blend the dates with the tahini and a splash of water until smooth and pourable.
Drizzle over bread, pancakes, or fresh fruit.

ingredients_full.ingredient: Seeds, sesame butter, tahini, from roasted and toasted kernels (most common type)

steps_detailed: Blend the dates with the tahini, cardamom, and a splash of water until smooth and pourable.
Drizzle over chebab or fresh fruit - the nutty tahini balances the dates' sweetness.
